articles.headline: India Unveils Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Focus on Exports and Skilling
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved Phase II of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, allocating an additional ₹25,000 crore for R&D and manufacturing incentives.
• This phase aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, leveraging its renewable energy potential.
• Key focus areas include developing infrastructure for hydrogen storage and transport, promoting green hydrogen adoption in heavy industries like steel and fertilizers, and establishing a robust skilling ecosystem.
• The mission targets a production capacity of 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030, reducing reliance on fossil fuels and generating significant employment.
• Incentives will be provided for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production under the SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) Programme.

articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a green hydrogen plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background, symbolizing renewable energy sources.
articles.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, resulting in zero greenhouse gas emissions.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Adjourns Hearing on Petitions Challenging Digital Personal Data Protection Act
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court today adjourned the hearing on a batch of petitions challenging various provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023, to the first week of December.
• Petitioners argue that certain clauses, particularly those related to government exemptions and data processing without consent in specific cases, infringe upon the fundamental right to privacy.
• The government, represented by the Attorney General, reiterated that the Act strikes a balance between individual privacy and legitimate state interests, essential for national security and public order.
• The DPDP Act aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data in India, establishing rights and duties of data principals and data fiduciaries.
• Key concerns revolve around the independence of the Data Protection Board and the broad powers granted to the central government.

mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional validity and implications of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act on individual privacy.

articles.trivia: India's journey towards a comprehensive data protection law began with the recommendations of the Justice B.N. Srikrishna Committee in 2018.

articles.headline: India-US Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Focus on Critical Technologies and Indo-Pacific Stability
articles.explainer:
• The latest round of the India-US Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, emphasizing deepening cooperation in critical and emerging technologies, defense, and clean energy.
• Both nations reaffirmed their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, expressing concerns over regional security challenges.
• Discussions included enhancing supply chain resilience, collaboration on semiconductors, AI, and quantum computing, and joint efforts in space exploration.
• The dialogue also covered counter-terrorism efforts and strengthening multilateral institutions to address global challenges.
• The two sides agreed on regular high-level exchanges and joint working group meetings to implement initiatives identified during the dialogue.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate, Focus on Inflation Management Amidst Global Uncertainties
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• RBI Governor highlighted global economic uncertainties, geopolitical tensions, and volatile crude oil prices as key risks to domestic inflation.
• Projections indicate retail inflation is expected to moderate but remains a concern, primarily due to food price volatility.
• The central bank emphasized close monitoring of liquidity conditions and the transmission of past rate hikes to sustain economic stability.

articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was established in 2016 and comprises six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: Union Cabinet Approves 'PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana' Expansion for Rural Households
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet has approved a significant expansion of the 'PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana', specifically targeting rural households across the country.
• The revamped scheme aims to provide free electricity to 1 crore rural households by installing rooftop solar panels, reducing their electricity bills to zero.
• It includes higher subsidies for rural beneficiaries, easier access to financing, and a robust awareness campaign to promote adoption in remote areas.
• The initiative is expected to boost rural employment through local manufacturing and installation of solar equipment, aligning with the Atmanirbhar Bharat vision.
• This expansion is projected to contribute significantly to India's renewable energy targets and reduce carbon emissions from conventional power sources.

articles.headline: ISRO Provides Update on Chandrayaan-4 Mission Preparations, Focus on Lunar Sample Return
articles.explainer:
• ISRO announced that preparations for the Chandrayaan-4 mission are progressing as per schedule, with key components undergoing rigorous testing.
• This ambitious mission aims to bring back lunar soil and rock samples to Earth for detailed scientific analysis, building upon the successes of Chandrayaan-2 and Chandrayaan-3.
• The mission involves a complex architecture with multiple modules, including a lander, ascender, orbiter, and a re-entry module, requiring advanced robotics and propulsion systems.
• Scientists anticipate that the returned samples will provide invaluable insights into the Moon's geological evolution, origin, and potential resources.
• India seeks to join a select group of nations capable of executing a lunar sample return mission, showcasing its growing prowess in deep space exploration.

articles.trivia: Only the US, Russia (then USSR), and China have successfully brought back samples from the Moon.

articles.headline: Air Quality Deteriorates in Delhi-NCR, Authorities Implement Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP) Stage III
articles.explainer:
• Air quality in Delhi-NCR significantly worsened, reaching 'severe' category in many areas, prompting authorities to implement Stage III of the Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP).
• GRAP Stage III includes measures such as banning non-essential construction and demolition activities, brick kilns, and hot mix plants, and imposing restrictions on BS-III petrol and BS-IV diesel vehicles.
• The primary factors contributing to the deterioration are identified as stubble burning in neighboring states, unfavorable meteorological conditions (low wind speed, temperature inversion), and local pollution sources.
• Public health advisories have been issued, urging residents to minimize outdoor activities and use masks.
• Efforts are underway to enforce pollution control norms and encourage shifting to public transport, but long-term solutions remain a challenge.

articles.trivia: PM2.5 particles are so small that they can penetrate deep into the lungs and even enter the bloodstream, posing significant health risks.

articles.headline: India and Brazil Hold High-Level Trade Talks, Eyeing Expansion in Agriculture and Pharma Sectors
articles.explainer:
• India and Brazil conducted high-level trade talks in Brasilia, aiming to diversify and expand their bilateral trade relationship.
• Discussions focused on increasing cooperation in agriculture, pharmaceuticals, energy, and digital technologies, with both sides keen on reducing trade barriers.
• Brazil, a major agricultural producer, expressed interest in India's pharmaceutical and IT expertise, while India sought better access for its agricultural exports.
• The two BRICS partners emphasized strengthening South-South cooperation and exploring new avenues for investment and joint ventures.
• A joint working group was formed to address specific trade issues and facilitate easier market access for products from both countries.

articles.headline: INS Vikrant Successfully Completes Critical Aviation Trials, Boosts India's Naval Prowess
articles.explainer:
• India's first indigenously-built aircraft carrier, INS Vikrant, has successfully completed a series of critical aviation trials, including the landing and take-off of various fighter jets.
• These trials demonstrate the carrier's full operational capability and its ability to integrate advanced aircraft, significantly enhancing India's naval power projection.
• The successful completion marks a major milestone for the 'Make in India' and Atmanirbhar Bharat initiatives in the defense sector.
• INS Vikrant, built by Cochin Shipyard Limited, is equipped with state-of-the-art systems and can operate a diverse air wing, including MiG-29K fighter jets and various helicopters.
• Its induction strengthens India's presence in the Indian Ocean Region and reinforces its status as a net security provider.

articles.headline: New Report Highlights Growing Threat of Urban Heat Islands in Indian Cities
articles.explainer:
• A recent report by environmental think tanks has warned about the increasing prevalence and intensity of urban heat islands (UHIs) across major Indian cities.
• UHIs occur when urban areas experience significantly higher temperatures than surrounding rural areas due to human activities and materials like concrete and asphalt that absorb and retain heat.
• The report identifies rapid urbanization, loss of green cover, and inadequate urban planning as primary drivers, leading to severe health risks, increased energy consumption, and reduced quality of life.
• Recommendations include promoting green infrastructure, using cool pavements and reflective surfaces, improving urban design, and developing heat action plans.
• The study emphasizes the urgent need for climate-resilient urban planning to mitigate the adverse effects of UHIs and ensure sustainable development.

articles.trivia: Atlanta, USA, is considered one of the most intense urban heat islands in the world due to its extensive concrete surfaces and limited green cover.

articles.headline: Government Reviews Progress of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Across States
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education held a high-level meeting to review the progress of implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 across various states and Union Territories.
• Emphasis was placed on foundational literacy and numeracy, multilingualism, skill development integration, and digital education initiatives.
• While some states have made significant strides, challenges persist in teacher training, infrastructure development, and curriculum reforms, particularly at the school level.
• The review highlighted the need for greater collaboration between central and state governments to ensure uniform and effective implementation of the policy's transformative vision.
• A roadmap for accelerating the remaining phases of NEP implementation, including higher education reforms, was discussed.

articles.headline: India Hosts Central Asia Dialogue, Reinforces Connectivity and Regional Security Ties
articles.explainer:
• India hosted the latest edition of the India-Central Asia Dialogue, bringing together foreign ministers from Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, and Uzbekistan.
• The dialogue focused on enhancing trade, investment, and cultural linkages, with a strong emphasis on developing reliable connectivity corridors, including the Chabahar Port.
• Regional security challenges, counter-terrorism cooperation, and the situation in Afghanistan were prominent topics of discussion.
• India reiterated its commitment to extending developmental assistance and technical expertise to Central Asian nations, particularly in areas of IT and healthcare.
• The joint statement highlighted shared concerns over extremism and pledged to work together for peace and prosperity in the extended neighborhood.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Discussion Paper on Ethical AI Framework for India
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog has released a comprehensive discussion paper outlining a proposed ethical framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) in India.
• The framework emphasizes principles like fairness, transparency, accountability, safety, and privacy in the development and deployment of AI systems.
• It seeks to foster responsible AI innovation while mitigating risks such as algorithmic bias, job displacement, and misuse of AI technologies.
• The paper invites public and stakeholder feedback to shape a robust and inclusive policy approach to AI governance.
• Key recommendations include establishing a multi-stakeholder body for AI ethics oversight and developing sector-specific guidelines for AI applications.

articles.headline: India Advocates for Greater Gender Inclusion in UN Peacekeeping Operations
articles.explainer:
• India, at a UN Security Council debate, strongly advocated for increasing the participation of women in all ranks of UN peacekeeping operations.
• India highlighted the critical role women peacekeepers play in improving mission effectiveness, fostering trust with local communities, and addressing gender-based violence.
• The Indian representative emphasized the need for adequate training, resources, and a safe working environment for female peacekeepers.
• India has historically been a significant contributor of troops to UN peacekeeping missions and has deployed all-women contingents in the past.
• The call aligns with the UN's Women, Peace and Security agenda, aiming to enhance women's roles in conflict prevention and resolution.

articles.headline: President Droupadi Murmu Inaugurates State-of-the-Art Tribal Freedom Fighters Museum in Jharkhand
articles.explainer:
• President Droupadi Murmu inaugurated a state-of-the-art Tribal Freedom Fighters Museum in Ranchi, Jharkhand, commemorating the contributions of tribal leaders to India's independence struggle.
• The museum aims to preserve and showcase the rich heritage, culture, and sacrifices of tribal communities, particularly their resistance against British colonial rule.
• It features interactive exhibits, historical artifacts, and multimedia presentations highlighting figures like Birsa Munda, Tilka Manjhi, and Sidhu-Kanhu.
• The initiative is part of the government's broader effort to recognize and integrate tribal history into the national narrative, promoting inclusivity and cultural pride.
• The museum is expected to become a significant educational and tourist destination, fostering a deeper understanding of tribal contributions.

articles.headline: G7 Nations Pledge Enhanced Climate Finance for Developing Countries at Bonn Climate Conference
articles.explainer:
• G7 nations collectively pledged to significantly increase their climate finance contributions to developing countries at the ongoing Bonn Climate Conference (COP29 preparatory meeting).
• The announcement aims to rebuild trust between developed and developing nations, crucial for achieving global climate goals and implementing the Paris Agreement.
• The finance is intended to support mitigation efforts (reducing emissions) and adaptation measures (coping with climate impacts) in vulnerable countries.
• Developing nations have consistently called for developed countries to meet their long-standing commitment of providing $100 billion annually in climate finance, a target often missed.
• The discussions also touched upon modalities for a new collective quantified goal (NCQG) on climate finance post-2025.

articles.headline: Nation Remembers Sir C.V. Raman on His Birth Anniversary, Celebrating Scientific Legacy
articles.explainer:
• Today marks the birth anniversary of Bharat Ratna Sir C.V. Raman, one of India's most distinguished physicists and Nobel laureate.
• Born on November 7, 1888, Raman is best known for his groundbreaking work on the scattering of light, leading to the discovery of the 'Raman Effect'.
• The Raman Effect, for which he was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1930, explains the change in the wavelength of light that occurs when a light beam is deflected by molecules.
• His discovery had profound implications for understanding the molecular structure of materials and has widespread applications in various scientific fields, including chemistry and biology.
• His legacy continues to inspire generations of Indian scientists and underscores India's historical contributions to fundamental science.

articles.headline: Scientists Discover New Species of Amphibian in Western Ghats, Highlights Biodiversity Hotspot Status
articles.explainer:
• Researchers from the Zoological Survey of India announced the discovery of a new species of amphibian in the highly biodiverse Western Ghats region.
• The discovery underscores the ecological richness and unique biodiversity of the Western Ghats, a designated UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the world's 36 biodiversity hotspots.
• The new species, a type of frog, was identified based on distinct morphological characteristics and genetic analysis, highlighting the need for continued exploration and conservation efforts.
• Scientists emphasize that many species in the Western Ghats remain undiscovered and face threats from habitat loss, climate change, and pollution.
• The finding reinforces the importance of protecting this fragile ecosystem, which is crucial for India's ecological balance and water security.

articles.headline: Report Indicates Significant Surge in Digital Payments Adoption in Tier-2 and Tier-3 Indian Cities
articles.explainer:
• A new report by a leading financial technology firm highlights a substantial increase in digital payment adoption and usage in India's Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
• The surge is attributed to government initiatives promoting digital transactions, widespread availability of smartphones, and the convenience offered by UPI (Unified Payments Interface).
• This trend signifies deeper financial inclusion, bringing more people into the formal economy and reducing reliance on cash transactions.
• The report notes a particular growth in merchant payments and small-value transactions, indicating a behavioral shift among consumers and businesses.
• Increased digital literacy campaigns and robust digital infrastructure are identified as key factors for sustaining this growth momentum.

Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life

Discuss how the widespread adoption of digital payments, particularly UPI, is transforming India's economy and society.
Evaluate the challenges that need to be addressed to ensure sustained and equitable growth of digital payments across all demographics.
Examine the role of government policies and technological innovations in fostering a cashless economy in India.
articles.imageDescription: A hand holding a smartphone scanning a QR code for payment at a small shop in a bustling Indian street, symbolizing digital transactions.
articles.trivia: UPI was launched in 2016 and has since become one of the most successful real-time payment systems globally, processing billions of transactions monthly.

articles.headline: GST Council Considers New Anti-Evasion Measures and Rationalization of Tax Rates
articles.explainer:
• The GST Council met to discuss new anti-evasion measures aimed at curbing fraudulent input tax credit (ITC) claims and ensuring greater compliance.
• Proposals include enhanced data analytics, stricter registration verification, and provisions for demanding security deposits from high-risk taxpayers.
• The Council also deliberated on rationalizing GST rates for certain goods and services to simplify the tax structure and address inverted duty structures.
• The objective is to boost revenue collection, reduce litigation, and make the Goods and Services Tax (GST) regime more efficient and taxpayer-friendly.
• Decisions from the meeting are expected to be announced soon, impacting businesses and consumers across various sectors.

Government Budgeting
Taxation

Discuss the challenges and benefits of rationalizing GST rates for various goods and services in India.
Evaluate the impact of GST reforms on businesses, consumers, and government revenue collection.
Examine how data analytics and technology can be leveraged to enhance GST compliance and minimize tax evasion.

articles.trivia: The Goods and Services Tax (GST) was implemented in India on July 1, 2017, subsuming multiple central and state taxes.

highlights: India launches Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, targeting global leadership in production and export.
Supreme Court adjourns hearing on petitions challenging Digital Personal Data Protection Act, sparking privacy debates.
India-US Strategic Dialogue concludes, focusing on critical technologies, defense, and Indo-Pacific security.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. The mission aims to achieve a production capacity of 10 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030.
2. The 'SIGHT Programme' provides incentives for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production.
3. Green hydrogen production involves splitting water using renewable electricity, resulting in zero greenhouse gas emissions.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. Phase II of the mission targets a production capacity of 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030. Statement 2 is correct, as the SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) Programme is a key component. Statement 3 is correct, as green hydrogen is produced through electrolysis powered by renewable energy, ensuring zero emissions.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following bodies is primarily responsible for implementing the Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP) to combat air pollution in Delhi-NCR?
options: (a) Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC)
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Commission for Air Quality Management (CAQM) in NCR and Adjoining Areas is primarily responsible for implementing the Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP). While MoEFCC sets policies and CPCB monitors, CAQM is the statutory body specifically tasked with coordinating and implementing measures for air quality management in the Delhi-NCR region.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023:
1. It aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data in India.
2. The Act does not allow for any exemptions for government agencies in data processing.
3. It establishes a Data Protection Board to enforce its provisions.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. The DPDP Act, 2023, is indeed designed to regulate the processing of digital personal data. Statement 2 is incorrect; one of the key concerns raised by petitioners is the broad exemptions provided to government agencies under certain circumstances. Statement 3 is correct; the Act provides for the establishment of a Data Protection Board to enforce its provisions.

prelimsMocks.question: With reference to India's space missions, the Chandrayaan-4 mission is primarily designed for which of the following objectives?
options: (a) To explore the Sun's outer corona and solar wind.
(b) To study Martian atmosphere and surface features.
(c) To bring back lunar soil and rock samples to Earth.
(d) To deploy a space telescope for exoplanet observation.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) To bring back lunar soil and rock samples to Earth.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Chandrayaan-4 mission is India's ambitious plan for a lunar sample return mission, aiming to collect and bring back lunar samples for scientific analysis. Option (a) describes Aditya-L1, (b) describes Mars Orbiter Mission, and (d) describes a general space telescope objective.

prelimsMocks.question: The term 'Urban Heat Island' (UHI) effect is most accurately described as:
options: (a) The phenomenon where urban areas experience significantly lower temperatures than surrounding rural areas.
(b) The effect of increased vegetation cover leading to cooler urban environments.
(c) The phenomenon where urban areas experience significantly higher temperatures than surrounding rural areas.
(d) The impact of industrial emissions causing localized atmospheric heating in rural areas.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) The phenomenon where urban areas experience significantly higher temperatures than surrounding rural areas.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Urban Heat Island (UHI) refers to the phenomenon where metropolitan areas are significantly warmer than their surrounding rural areas due to human activities such as building construction, lack of green spaces, and heat generated by energy consumption. Options (a) and (b) describe the opposite or mitigating factors, while (d) is not the primary definition of UHI.

mainsMocks.question: Critically analyze the potential of India's National Green Hydrogen Mission to achieve energy security and contribute to global decarbonization efforts. What challenges must be addressed for its successful implementation?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Infrastructure: Energy; Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation; Science and Technology-developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the National Green Hydrogen Mission and its objectives. Discuss its potential for energy security (reducing import dependence) and decarbonization (clean fuel). Identify benefits like economic growth, job creation, and export potential. Then, delve into challenges: high production costs, need for significant renewable energy capacity, infrastructure for storage and transport, technological advancements, and international competition. Conclude with policy recommendations for successful implementation.

mainsMocks.question: The Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, has been a subject of intense debate regarding its balance between individual privacy and legitimate state interests. Discuss the key provisions of the Act that raise concerns about privacy and suggest measures to strengthen data protection while safeguarding national security.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; Important aspects of governance, transparency and accountability; Indian Constitution—significant provisions and basic structure.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by outlining the intent of the DPDP Act. Discuss its core principles (consent, data minimization, accountability). Then, critically analyze provisions that raise privacy concerns, such as broad exemptions for government agencies, processing without consent in certain cases, and the independence of the Data Protection Board. Relate these to the fundamental right to privacy (Puttaswamy judgment). In the second part, suggest measures like clearer definitions for 'legitimate uses', robust oversight mechanisms for government data access, strengthening the Data Protection Board's autonomy, and ensuring transparency in data processing activities to strike a better balance.

mainsMocks.question: Despite concerted efforts, air pollution in Delhi-NCR remains a perennial public health crisis. Examine the multifaceted causes of this recurring problem and critically evaluate the effectiveness of the Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP) in mitigating it. What long-term, sustainable solutions are required?
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Disaster Management; Urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
mainsMocks.approach: Start by describing the severity of air pollution in Delhi-NCR and its health impacts. Detail the multifaceted causes, including stubble burning, vehicular emissions, industrial pollution, construction dust, and meteorological factors (temperature inversion, low wind speed). Explain GRAP's structure and its measures (Stages I-IV). Critically evaluate GRAP's effectiveness – its short-term reactive nature, implementation challenges, and limited impact on root causes. Conclude by proposing long-term sustainable solutions: transitioning to cleaner fuels, promoting public transport, adopting sustainable agricultural practices, green infrastructure, inter-state cooperation, robust waste management, and public awareness.

GS1.headline: President Droupadi Murmu Inaugurates State-of-the-Art Tribal Freedom Fighters Museum in Jharkhand
GS1.explainer:
• President Droupadi Murmu inaugurated a state-of-the-art Tribal Freedom Fighters Museum in Ranchi, Jharkhand, commemorating the contributions of tribal leaders to India's independence struggle.
• The museum aims to preserve and showcase the rich heritage, culture, and sacrifices of tribal communities, particularly their resistance against British colonial rule.
• It features interactive exhibits, historical artifacts, and multimedia presentations highlighting figures like Birsa Munda, Tilka Manjhi, and Sidhu-Kanhu.
• The initiative is part of the government's broader effort to recognize and integrate tribal history into the national narrative, promoting inclusivity and cultural pride.
• The museum is expected to become a significant educational and tourist destination, fostering a deeper understanding of tribal contributions.

Examine the challenges faced by tribal communities during colonial rule and their unique forms of resistance.

GS1.trivia: Birsa Munda, often referred to as 'Dharti Abba' (Father of the Earth), led a tribal religious-political movement in the late 19th century against British rule and missionary activities.

GS1.headline: Nation Remembers Sir C.V. Raman on His Birth Anniversary, Celebrating Scientific Legacy
GS1.explainer:
• Today marks the birth anniversary of Bharat Ratna Sir C.V. Raman, one of India's most distinguished physicists and Nobel laureate.
• Born on November 7, 1888, Raman is best known for his groundbreaking work on the scattering of light, leading to the discovery of the 'Raman Effect'.
• The Raman Effect, for which he was awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics in 1930, explains the change in the wavelength of light that occurs when a light beam is deflected by molecules.
• His discovery had profound implications for understanding the molecular structure of materials and has widespread applications in various scientific fields, including chemistry and biology.
• His legacy continues to inspire generations of Indian scientists and underscores India's historical contributions to fundamental science.

GS1.imageDescription: A portrait of Sir C.V. Raman, with a diagram illustrating the scattering of light and spectral lines in the background.
GS1.trivia: National Science Day in India is celebrated on February 28 each year to commemorate the discovery of the Raman Effect.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Adjourns Hearing on Petitions Challenging Digital Personal Data Protection Act
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court today adjourned the hearing on a batch of petitions challenging various provisions of the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act), 2023, to the first week of December.
• Petitioners argue that certain clauses, particularly those related to government exemptions and data processing without consent in specific cases, infringe upon the fundamental right to privacy.
• The government, represented by the Attorney General, reiterated that the Act strikes a balance between individual privacy and legitimate state interests, essential for national security and public order.
• The DPDP Act aims to regulate the processing of digital personal data in India, establishing rights and duties of data principals and data fiduciaries.
• Key concerns revolve around the independence of the Data Protection Board and the broad powers granted to the central government.

Critically evaluate the provisions of the DPDP Act concerning government exemptions and their potential for misuse.

GS2.trivia: India's journey towards a comprehensive data protection law began with the recommendations of the Justice B.N. Srikrishna Committee in 2018.

GS2.headline: India-US Strategic Dialogue Concludes, Focus on Critical Technologies and Indo-Pacific Stability
GS2.explainer:
• The latest round of the India-US Strategic Dialogue concluded in New Delhi, emphasizing deepening cooperation in critical and emerging technologies, defense, and clean energy.
• Both nations reaffirmed their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, expressing concerns over regional security challenges.
• Discussions included enhancing supply chain resilience, collaboration on semiconductors, AI, and quantum computing, and joint efforts in space exploration.
• The dialogue also covered counter-terrorism efforts and strengthening multilateral institutions to address global challenges.
• The two sides agreed on regular high-level exchanges and joint working group meetings to implement initiatives identified during the dialogue.

GS2.headline: India and Brazil Hold High-Level Trade Talks, Eyeing Expansion in Agriculture and Pharma Sectors
GS2.explainer:
• India and Brazil conducted high-level trade talks in Brasilia, aiming to diversify and expand their bilateral trade relationship.
• Discussions focused on increasing cooperation in agriculture, pharmaceuticals, energy, and digital technologies, with both sides keen on reducing trade barriers.
• Brazil, a major agricultural producer, expressed interest in India's pharmaceutical and IT expertise, while India sought better access for its agricultural exports.
• The two BRICS partners emphasized strengthening South-South cooperation and exploring new avenues for investment and joint ventures.
• A joint working group was formed to address specific trade issues and facilitate easier market access for products from both countries.

GS2.headline: Government Reviews Progress of National Education Policy 2020 Implementation Across States
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education held a high-level meeting to review the progress of implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 across various states and Union Territories.
• Emphasis was placed on foundational literacy and numeracy, multilingualism, skill development integration, and digital education initiatives.
• While some states have made significant strides, challenges persist in teacher training, infrastructure development, and curriculum reforms, particularly at the school level.
• The review highlighted the need for greater collaboration between central and state governments to ensure uniform and effective implementation of the policy's transformative vision.
• A roadmap for accelerating the remaining phases of NEP implementation, including higher education reforms, was discussed.

GS2.headline: India Hosts Central Asia Dialogue, Reinforces Connectivity and Regional Security Ties
GS2.explainer:
• India hosted the latest edition of the India-Central Asia Dialogue, bringing together foreign ministers from Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan, and Uzbekistan.
• The dialogue focused on enhancing trade, investment, and cultural linkages, with a strong emphasis on developing reliable connectivity corridors, including the Chabahar Port.
• Regional security challenges, counter-terrorism cooperation, and the situation in Afghanistan were prominent topics of discussion.
• India reiterated its commitment to extending developmental assistance and technical expertise to Central Asian nations, particularly in areas of IT and healthcare.
• The joint statement highlighted shared concerns over extremism and pledged to work together for peace and prosperity in the extended neighborhood.

GS2.headline: India Advocates for Greater Gender Inclusion in UN Peacekeeping Operations
GS2.explainer:
• India, at a UN Security Council debate, strongly advocated for increasing the participation of women in all ranks of UN peacekeeping operations.
• India highlighted the critical role women peacekeepers play in improving mission effectiveness, fostering trust with local communities, and addressing gender-based violence.
• The Indian representative emphasized the need for adequate training, resources, and a safe working environment for female peacekeepers.
• India has historically been a significant contributor of troops to UN peacekeeping missions and has deployed all-women contingents in the past.
• The call aligns with the UN's Women, Peace and Security agenda, aiming to enhance women's roles in conflict prevention and resolution.

GS3.headline: India Unveils Phase II of National Green Hydrogen Mission, Focus on Exports and Skilling
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet approved Phase II of the National Green Hydrogen Mission, allocating an additional ₹25,000 crore for R&D and manufacturing incentives.
• This phase aims to position India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, leveraging its renewable energy potential.
• Key focus areas include developing infrastructure for hydrogen storage and transport, promoting green hydrogen adoption in heavy industries like steel and fertilizers, and establishing a robust skilling ecosystem.
• The mission targets a production capacity of 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030, reducing reliance on fossil fuels and generating significant employment.
• Incentives will be provided for electrolyser manufacturing and green hydrogen production under the SIGHT (Strategic Interventions for Green Hydrogen Transition) Programme.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate, Focus on Inflation Management Amidst Global Uncertainties
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) unanimously decided to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5% for the sixth consecutive time.
• The MPC reiterated its commitment to 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation aligns with the target while supporting growth.
• RBI Governor highlighted global economic uncertainties, geopolitical tensions, and volatile crude oil prices as key risks to domestic inflation.
• Projections indicate retail inflation is expected to moderate but remains a concern, primarily due to food price volatility.
• The central bank emphasized close monitoring of liquidity conditions and the transmission of past rate hikes to sustain economic stability.

GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) was established in 2016 and comprises six members – three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: Union Cabinet Approves 'PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana' Expansion for Rural Households
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet has approved a significant expansion of the 'PM Surya Ghar: Muft Bijli Yojana', specifically targeting rural households across the country.
• The revamped scheme aims to provide free electricity to 1 crore rural households by installing rooftop solar panels, reducing their electricity bills to zero.
• It includes higher subsidies for rural beneficiaries, easier access to financing, and a robust awareness campaign to promote adoption in remote areas.
• The initiative is expected to boost rural employment through local manufacturing and installation of solar equipment, aligning with the Atmanirbhar Bharat vision.
• This expansion is projected to contribute significantly to India's renewable energy targets and reduce carbon emissions from conventional power sources.

GS3.headline: ISRO Provides Update on Chandrayaan-4 Mission Preparations, Focus on Lunar Sample Return
GS3.explainer:
• ISRO announced that preparations for the Chandrayaan-4 mission are progressing as per schedule, with key components undergoing rigorous testing.
• This ambitious mission aims to bring back lunar soil and rock samples to Earth for detailed scientific analysis, building upon the successes of Chandrayaan-2 and Chandrayaan-3.
• The mission involves a complex architecture with multiple modules, including a lander, ascender, orbiter, and a re-entry module, requiring advanced robotics and propulsion systems.
• Scientists anticipate that the returned samples will provide invaluable insights into the Moon's geological evolution, origin, and potential resources.
• India seeks to join a select group of nations capable of executing a lunar sample return mission, showcasing its growing prowess in deep space exploration.

Examine the potential economic benefits derived from advancements in space technology.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of the Chandrayaan-4 spacecraft on the lunar surface, with a robotic arm collecting samples.
GS3.trivia: Only the US, Russia (then USSR), and China have successfully brought back samples from the Moon.

GS3.headline: Air Quality Deteriorates in Delhi-NCR, Authorities Implement Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP) Stage III
GS3.explainer:
• Air quality in Delhi-NCR significantly worsened, reaching 'severe' category in many areas, prompting authorities to implement Stage III of the Graded Response Action Plan (GRAP).
• GRAP Stage III includes measures such as banning non-essential construction and demolition activities, brick kilns, and hot mix plants, and imposing restrictions on BS-III petrol and BS-IV diesel vehicles.
• The primary factors contributing to the deterioration are identified as stubble burning in neighboring states, unfavorable meteorological conditions (low wind speed, temperature inversion), and local pollution sources.
• Public health advisories have been issued, urging residents to minimize outdoor activities and use masks.
• Efforts are underway to enforce pollution control norms and encourage shifting to public transport, but long-term solutions remain a challenge.

GS3.trivia: PM2.5 particles are so small that they can penetrate deep into the lungs and even enter the bloodstream, posing significant health risks.

GS3.headline: INS Vikrant Successfully Completes Critical Aviation Trials, Boosts India's Naval Prowess
GS3.explainer:
• India's first indigenously-built aircraft carrier, INS Vikrant, has successfully completed a series of critical aviation trials, including the landing and take-off of various fighter jets.
• These trials demonstrate the carrier's full operational capability and its ability to integrate advanced aircraft, significantly enhancing India's naval power projection.
• The successful completion marks a major milestone for the 'Make in India' and Atmanirbhar Bharat initiatives in the defense sector.
• INS Vikrant, built by Cochin Shipyard Limited, is equipped with state-of-the-art systems and can operate a diverse air wing, including MiG-29K fighter jets and various helicopters.
• Its induction strengthens India's presence in the Indian Ocean Region and reinforces its status as a net security provider.

GS3.trivia: INS Vikrant is named after India's first aircraft carrier, which was acquired from the UK and served the Indian Navy from 1961 to 1997.

GS3.headline: New Report Highlights Growing Threat of Urban Heat Islands in Indian Cities
GS3.explainer:
• A recent report by environmental think tanks has warned about the increasing prevalence and intensity of urban heat islands (UHIs) across major Indian cities.
• UHIs occur when urban areas experience significantly higher temperatures than surrounding rural areas due to human activities and materials like concrete and asphalt that absorb and retain heat.
• The report identifies rapid urbanization, loss of green cover, and inadequate urban planning as primary drivers, leading to severe health risks, increased energy consumption, and reduced quality of life.
• Recommendations include promoting green infrastructure, using cool pavements and reflective surfaces, improving urban design, and developing heat action plans.
• The study emphasizes the urgent need for climate-resilient urban planning to mitigate the adverse effects of UHIs and ensure sustainable development.

GS3.trivia: Atlanta, USA, is considered one of the most intense urban heat islands in the world due to its extensive concrete surfaces and limited green cover.

GS3.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Discussion Paper on Ethical AI Framework for India
GS3.explainer:
• NITI Aayog has released a comprehensive discussion paper outlining a proposed ethical framework for Artificial Intelligence (AI) in India.
• The framework emphasizes principles like fairness, transparency, accountability, safety, and privacy in the development and deployment of AI systems.
• It seeks to foster responsible AI innovation while mitigating risks such as algorithmic bias, job displacement, and misuse of AI technologies.
• The paper invites public and stakeholder feedback to shape a robust and inclusive policy approach to AI governance.
• Key recommendations include establishing a multi-stakeholder body for AI ethics oversight and developing sector-specific guidelines for AI applications.

GS3.trivia: AI is often categorized into Narrow AI (ANI), General AI (AGI), and Super AI (ASI), with most current applications falling under Narrow AI.

GS3.headline: G7 Nations Pledge Enhanced Climate Finance for Developing Countries at Bonn Climate Conference
GS3.explainer:
• G7 nations collectively pledged to significantly increase their climate finance contributions to developing countries at the ongoing Bonn Climate Conference (COP29 preparatory meeting).
• The announcement aims to rebuild trust between developed and developing nations, crucial for achieving global climate goals and implementing the Paris Agreement.
• The finance is intended to support mitigation efforts (reducing emissions) and adaptation measures (coping with climate impacts) in vulnerable countries.
• Developing nations have consistently called for developed countries to meet their long-standing commitment of providing $100 billion annually in climate finance, a target often missed.
• The discussions also touched upon modalities for a new collective quantified goal (NCQG) on climate finance post-2025.

GS3.headline: Scientists Discover New Species of Amphibian in Western Ghats, Highlights Biodiversity Hotspot Status
GS3.explainer:
• Researchers from the Zoological Survey of India announced the discovery of a new species of amphibian in the highly biodiverse Western Ghats region.
• The discovery underscores the ecological richness and unique biodiversity of the Western Ghats, a designated UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the world's 36 biodiversity hotspots.
• The new species, a type of frog, was identified based on distinct morphological characteristics and genetic analysis, highlighting the need for continued exploration and conservation efforts.
• Scientists emphasize that many species in the Western Ghats remain undiscovered and face threats from habitat loss, climate change, and pollution.
• The finding reinforces the importance of protecting this fragile ecosystem, which is crucial for India's ecological balance and water security.

GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats is home to at least 325 globally threatened species, including 229 plant species, 31 mammal species, 15 bird species, 43 amphibian species, 5 reptile species and 1 fish species.

GS3.headline: Report Indicates Significant Surge in Digital Payments Adoption in Tier-2 and Tier-3 Indian Cities
GS3.explainer:
• A new report by a leading financial technology firm highlights a substantial increase in digital payment adoption and usage in India's Tier-2 and Tier-3 cities.
• The surge is attributed to government initiatives promoting digital transactions, widespread availability of smartphones, and the convenience offered by UPI (Unified Payments Interface).
• This trend signifies deeper financial inclusion, bringing more people into the formal economy and reducing reliance on cash transactions.
• The report notes a particular growth in merchant payments and small-value transactions, indicating a behavioral shift among consumers and businesses.
• Increased digital literacy campaigns and robust digital infrastructure are identified as key factors for sustaining this growth momentum.

GS3.headline: GST Council Considers New Anti-Evasion Measures and Rationalization of Tax Rates
GS3.explainer:
• The GST Council met to discuss new anti-evasion measures aimed at curbing fraudulent input tax credit (ITC) claims and ensuring greater compliance.
• Proposals include enhanced data analytics, stricter registration verification, and provisions for demanding security deposits from high-risk taxpayers.
• The Council also deliberated on rationalizing GST rates for certain goods and services to simplify the tax structure and address inverted duty structures.
• The objective is to boost revenue collection, reduce litigation, and make the Goods and Services Tax (GST) regime more efficient and taxpayer-friendly.
• Decisions from the meeting are expected to be announced soon, impacting businesses and consumers across various sectors.
